<a id="t-b57da2b4ff"></a>
## Thread: Precompiler Microfocus and Oracle — 1 msgs

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers) · [`Databases and SQL`](topics.md#databases)_

### Precompiler Microfocus and Oracle

- **From:** Johan den Boer <jj.den.boer@hccnet.nl>
- **Date:** 1999-06-12T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3761F5CC.E6A57817@hccnet.nl>`

```
Hi,

Is there a difference between compiling with cobsql from microfocus and
procob from Oracle.
I have some strange things in my cobol programs. I use embedded sql from

oracle in my cobol sources and I have problems with the declarations of
the
cobol variables which access the database tables.
Suppose a table has a field myfield number(6) in oracle. In one program
I had to declare the host variables as :

01    ws-myfield        pic s9(06) comp.

and in an other program I had to declare it as

01    ws-myfield        pic s9(07) comp.

If take the same declarations for ws-myfield in program B from program A
I don't
get the right answers from my sql query ?

Does anyone have the solution. Is there a standard way to declare
your cobol host variables.

regards : Johan den Boer

email : jj.den.boer@hccnet.nl
```

---

<a id="t-230e180b10"></a>
## Thread: Canceling .dlls — 2 msgs

### Canceling .dlls

- **From:** "Bill Wood" <beavis27@email.msn.com>
- **Date:** 1999-06-12T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<O1oh0aOt#GA.197@cpmsnbbsa03>`

```
I am using NetExpress 3.0 and I'm having trouble canceling .dlls after I'm
done with them.  I am using a pointer to the .dll during the initialization
of my progam.  After finishing and canceling all of the programs that I have
used in the .dll I am canceling it using CANCEL "dllname" but I only regain
a little bit of memory -- certainly not what the .dll took to start with.
Any suggestions?

bill
```

#### ↳ Re: Canceling .dlls

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 1999-06-12T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7ju89j$jdo@dfw-ixnews6.ix.netcom.com>`
- **References:** `<O1oh0aOt#GA.197@cpmsnbbsa03>`

```
I can't remember which it is right now, but I think there is either a
run-time switch or possibly a compiler option that tells "CANCEL" to free
storage.  (The ANSI Standard has never required this.)  Check out your MF
LRM - and at the beginning of the CANCEL statement I think there is a list
of compiler options and run-time options that impact its behavior.
```

---

<a id="t-85b9740700"></a>
## Thread: help required — 16 msgs

_Topics: [`Help requests and how-to`](topics.md#help)_

### help required

- **From:** "Stuart Ward" <stuart@printech.force9.co.uk>
- **Date:** 1999-06-13T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<XlQ83.3437$Hy4.2877@wards>`

```
Hi
This program is part of the computeach course in systems analysis, for
anyone familier it is exercise 5 question b. I having trouble with the maths
part of the prog it wont update the balance field correctly as the least sig
digit gets corrupted when adding. and see below for subtracting.
the data under the "XX" should be 09 not 0I . I'm a bit stumped on this and
i'd apprecite any help

Cheers

Stuart Ward
West Yorkshire

IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
  PROGRAM-ID. QUESTION-B.

  ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
  INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION.
  FILE-CONTROL.
  SELECT TRANSFILE ASSIGN TO "TRANS.DAT"
   ORGANIZATION LINE SEQUENTIAL.
  SELECT STOCKMAST ASSIGN TO "STOCKMA.DAT"
   ORGANIZATION LINE SEQUENTIAL.
  SELECT NEW-STOCKMAS ASSIGN TO "NSTOCKMA.DAT"
   ORGANIZATION LINE SEQUENTIAL.
  SELECT QUERYFILE ASSIGN TO "QUERY.DAT"
   ORGANIZATION LINE SEQUENTIAL.
  SELECT PRINT-FILE ASSIGN TO PRINTER.

  DATA DIVISION.
  FILE SECTION.
  FD TRANSFILE.
  01 TRANS-REC.
         03 PART-NUM  PIC X(8).
   88 END-OF-TRANS VALUE HIGH-VALUES.
  03 TRANS-TYPE  PIC X.
         03 C-S-NUM   PIC 9(6).
  03 TRANS-DATE  PIC 9(6).
  03 QUANTITY  PIC 9(6).

  FD STOCKMAST.
  01 STOCKM-REC.
  03 M-PART-NUM  PIC X(8).
   88 END-OF-MAST VALUE HIGH-VALUES.
  03 L-TRANS-DATE  PIC 9(6).
  03 BALANCE   PIC S9(6).
  03 MIN   PIC 9(6).
  03 MAX   PIC 9(6).

  FD QUERYFILE.
  01 QUERY-REC   PIC X(33).

  FD NEW-STOCKMAS.
  01 N-STOCK-REC   PIC X(33).

  FD PRINT-FILE
  LINAGE IS 60 LINES
  WITH FOOTING AT 56
  LINES AT TOP 2
  LINES AT BOTTOM 4.
  01 PRINT-REC   PIC X(68).

  WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.


  01 W-MAIN-HEAD.
         03    PIC X(34) VALUE SPACES.
         03    PIC X(12) VALUE
                 "ERROR REPORT".

  01 W-SUB-HEAD.
  03    PIC XXX VALUE SPACES.
  03    PIC X(12) VALUE
   "PART NO.".
  03    PIC X(13) VALUE
   "TRANS TYPE".
  03    PIC X(17) VALUE
   "CUST/SUPP NO.".
  03    PIC X(14) VALUE
   "TRANS DATE".
  03    PIC X(8) VALUE
   "QUANTITY".

  01 W-DETAIL.
  03    PIC XXX VALUE SPACES.
  03 P-PART-NUM  PIC X(8).
  03    PIC X(8) VALUE SPACES.
  03 P-TRANS-TYPE  PIC X.
  03    PIC X(11) VALUE SPACES.
  03 P-C-S-NUM  PIC Z(5)9.
  03    PIC X(10) VALUE SPACES.
  03 P-TRANS-DATE  PIC Z(5)9.
  03    PIC X(7) VALUE SPACES.
  03 P-QUANTITY  PIC Z(5)9.

  PROCEDURE DIVISION.
  MAIN-CONTROL.
  PERFORM INITIAL-PROCESS
  PERFORM UPDATE-PROCESS UNTIL
   END-OF-TRANS AND END-OF-MAST
  PERFORM FINAL-PROCESS
  STOP RUN.

  INITIAL-PROCESS.
  OPEN  INPUT  TRANSFILE
    STOCKMAST
   OUTPUT NEW-STOCKMAS
    QUERYFILE
    PRINT-FILE
  PERFORM READ-TRANSFILE
  PERFORM READ-STOCKMAST.
/  PERFORM NEW-HEADINGS.

  UPDATE-PROCESS.
   EVALUATE TRUE
    WHEN PART-NUM < M-PART-NUM
/     PERFORM PRINT-DETAIL
     PERFORM READ-TRANSFILE
    WHEN PART-NUM > M-PART-NUM
            WRITE N-STOCK-REC FROM STOCKM-REC
     PERFORM READ-STOCKMAST
    WHEN PART-NUM = M-PART-NUM
     PERFORM CALCULATE-DATA
     PERFORM READ-TRANSFILE
   END-EVALUATE.

  CALCULATE-DATA.
   MOVE TRANS-DATE TO L-TRANS-DATE
   IF TRANS-TYPE = "R" THEN
    COMPUTE BALANCE = BALANCE + QUANTITY
    ELSE IF TRANS-TYPE = "I" THEN
     COMPUTE BALANCE = BALANCE - QUANTITY
    END-IF
   END-IF
   IF BALANCE < MIN OR BALANCE > MAX THEN
    WRITE QUERY-REC FROM STOCKM-REC
   END-IF.

  READ-TRANSFILE.
   READ TRANSFILE AT END
    MOVE HIGH-VALUES TO PART-NUM
   END-READ.

  READ-STOCKMAST.
   READ STOCKMAST AT END
    MOVE HIGH-VALUES TO M-PART-NUM
   END-READ.

  NEW-HEADINGS.
          WRITE PRINT-REC FROM W-MAIN-HEAD AFTER PAGE
   WRITE PRINT-REC FROM W-SUB-HEAD AFTER 3.

  PRINT-DETAIL.
   MOVE TRANS-TYPE TO P-TRANS-TYPE
   MOVE PART-NUM TO P-PART-NUM
   MOVE C-S-NUM TO P-C-S-NUM
   MOVE TRANS-DATE TO P-TRANS-DATE
   MOVE QUANTITY TO P-QUANTITY
   WRITE PRINT-REC FROM W-DETAIL AFTER 1
    AT END-OF-PAGE
     PERFORM NEW-HEADINGS
   END-WRITE.

  FINAL-PROCESS.
   CLOSE TRANSFILE
    STOCKMAST
    NEW-STOCKMAS
    QUERYFILE
/    PRINT-FILE.


TRANS.DAT

00001234I001234010299000001


STOCKMA.DAT

00001234010199000010000005000100
00001235010199000125000010001000
00001258010199005212000100010000
00022154010199000123000010001000
00022164010199000125000025000250


NSTOCKMA.DAT (OUTPUT FILE  NEW MASTER)
                                        XX
0000123401029900000I000005000100
00001235010199000125000010001000
00001258010199005212000100010000
00022154010199000123000010001000
00022164010199000125000025000250

XX SHOULD BE 09
```

#### ↳ Re: help required

- **From:** "Leif Svalgaard" <lsvalg@ibm.net>
- **Date:** 1999-06-13T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3763e3fe@news3.us.ibm.net>`
- **References:** `<XlQ83.3437$Hy4.2877@wards>`

```
Stuart Ward <stuart@printech.force9.co.uk> wrote in
>it wont update the balance field correctly as the least sig
>digit gets corrupted when adding. and see below for subtracting.
…[11 more quoted lines elided]…
> XX SHOULD BE 09

There is nothing wrong !
Balance is SIGNED. And 'I' is the 9th signed digit. This is
the way YOUR compiler stores signed digits. Other compilers
do it differently.
Think about what you would expect if the balance were -9
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: help required

- **From:** docdwarf@clark.net ()
- **Date:** 1999-06-13T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<yNR83.497$S2.33693@iad-read.news.verio.net>`
- **References:** `<XlQ83.3437$Hy4.2877@wards> <3763e3fe@news3.us.ibm.net>`

```
In article <3763e3fe@news3.us.ibm.net>, Leif Svalgaard <leif@ibm.net> wrote:

[snippolinio]

>There is nothing wrong !
>Balance is SIGNED. And 'I' is the 9th signed digit. This is
>the way YOUR compiler stores signed digits. Other compilers
>do it differently.
>Think about what you would expect if the balance were -9

You beat me to it, Mr Svalgaard... but let me say that *this* is how one
should, in my opinion, request assistance on a programming problem in
order to eliminate the risk of being told to do one's own homework.

DD
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: help required

- **From:** "Stuart Ward" <stuart@printech.force9.co.uk>
- **Date:** 1999-06-13T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<vfU83.3480$Hy4.3040@wards>`
- **References:** `<XlQ83.3437$Hy4.2877@wards> <3763e3fe@news3.us.ibm.net> <yNR83.497$S2.33693@iad-read.news.verio.net>`

```
Hi
New problem now, I dont seem to be able to read a second matching record, it
just ignores it. It's proberbly somthing silly but I thought I'd got the
loop correct. Any help would be appreciated (please no complete solutions if
poss).
The second line of data in the trans file is ignored unlee it does not match
the master file in which case it prints as it should.

Cheers

Stuart Ward
West Yorkshire

   "TRANS TYPE".

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: help required

_(thread depth: 4)_

- **From:** dxmixxer@netdirect.net (Doug Miller)
- **Date:** 1999-06-14T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7k1rhp$168_003@news.netdirect.net>`
- **References:** `<XlQ83.3437$Hy4.2877@wards> <3763e3fe@news3.us.ibm.net> <yNR83.497$S2.33693@iad-read.news.verio.net> <vfU83.3480$Hy4.3040@wards>`

```
In article <vfU83.3480$Hy4.3040@wards>,
   "Stuart Ward" <stuart@printech.force9.co.uk> wrote:
+Hi
+New problem now, I dont seem to be able to read a second matching record, it
+just ignores it. It's proberbly somthing silly but I thought I'd got the
+loop correct. Any help would be appreciated (please no complete solutions if
+poss).
+The second line of data in the trans file is ignored unlee it does not match
+the master file in which case it prints as it should.
+
When the transaction record and the master record match,
after processing the transaction you need to read a new
record from the transaction file *and* from the master
file. You're reading only the transaction file.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: help required

_(thread depth: 5)_

- **From:** "Stuart Ward" <stuart@printech.force9.co.uk>
- **Date:** 1999-06-15T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7Uo93.3854$Hy4.3374@wards>`
- **References:** `<XlQ83.3437$Hy4.2877@wards> <3763e3fe@news3.us.ibm.net> <yNR83.497$S2.33693@iad-read.news.verio.net> <vfU83.3480$Hy4.3040@wards> <7k1rhp$168_003@news.netdirect.net>`

```

Cheers Doug but that is taken care of by this section

TRANSFILE
    WHEN PART-NUM > M-PART-NUM
            WRITE N-STOCK-REC FROM STOCKM-REC
     PERFORM READ-STOCKMAST

I just relised (4am) monday morning that the code was ok and it was my test
data that was sorted incorrectly (what a plonker) still no erros is not too
bad. here's the full listing that I'm submitting today. Any comments about
the quality of the solution/code would be very helpful (please ignore
formatting it looks great in fujitsu).

Cheers

Stuart Ward
West Yorkshire

 IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
 PROGRAM-ID. QUESTION-B.

 ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
 INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION.
 FILE-CONTROL.
  SELECT TRANSFILE ASSIGN TO "TRANS.DAT"
   ORGANIZATION LINE SEQUENTIAL.
  SELECT STOCKMAST ASSIGN TO "STOCKMA.DAT"
   ORGANIZATION LINE SEQUENTIAL.
  SELECT NEW-STOCKMAS ASSIGN TO "NSTOCKMA.DAT"
   ORGANIZATION LINE SEQUENTIAL.
  SELECT QUERYFILE ASSIGN TO "QUERY.DAT"
   ORGANIZATION LINE SEQUENTIAL.
  SELECT PRINT-FILE ASSIGN TO PRINTER.

 DATA DIVISION.
 FILE SECTION.
 FD TRANSFILE.
 01 TRANS-REC.
         03 PART-NUM  PIC X(8).
   88 END-OF-TRANS VALUE HIGH-VALUES.
  03 TRANS-TYPE  PIC X.
         03 C-S-NUM   PIC 9(6).
  03 TRANS-DATE  PIC 9(6).
  03 QUANTITY  PIC 9(6).

 FD STOCKMAST.
 01 STOCKM-REC.
  03 M-PART-NUM  PIC X(8).
   88 END-OF-MAST VALUE HIGH-VALUES.
  03 L-TRANS-DATE  PIC 9(6).
  03 BALANCE   PIC S9(6).
  03 MIN   PIC 9(6).
  03 MAX   PIC 9(6).

 FD QUERYFILE.
 01 QUERY-REC   PIC X(33).

 FD NEW-STOCKMAS.
 01 N-STOCK-REC   PIC X(33).

 FD PRINT-FILE
  LINAGE IS 60 LINES
  WITH FOOTING AT 56
  LINES AT TOP 2
  LINES AT BOTTOM 4.
 01 PRINT-REC   PIC X(71).

 WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.


 01 W-MAIN-HEAD.
         03    PIC X(34) VALUE SPACES.
         03    PIC X(12) VALUE
                 "ERROR REPORT".

 01 W-SUB-HEAD.
  03    PIC X(5) VALUE SPACES.
  03    PIC X(12) VALUE
   "PART NO.".
  03    PIC X(13) VALUE
   "TRANS TYPE".
  03    PIC X(17) VALUE
   "CUST/SUPP NO.".
  03    PIC X(14) VALUE
   "TRANS DATE".
  03    PIC X(8) VALUE
   "QUANTITY".

 01 W-DETAIL.
  03    PIC X(5) VALUE SPACES.
  03 P-PART-NUM  PIC X(8).
  03    PIC X(8) VALUE SPACES.
  03 P-TRANS-TYPE  PIC X.
  03    PIC X(11) VALUE SPACES.
  03 P-C-S-NUM  PIC X(6).
  03    PIC X(10) VALUE SPACES.
  03 P-TRANS-DATE  PIC X(6).
  03    PIC X(7) VALUE SPACES.
  03 P-QUANTITY  PIC Z(6)9.

 PROCEDURE DIVISION.
 MAIN-CONTROL.
  PERFORM INITIAL-PROCESS
  PERFORM UPDATE-PROCESS UNTIL
   END-OF-TRANS AND END-OF-MAST
  PERFORM FINAL-PROCESS
  STOP RUN.

 INITIAL-PROCESS.
  OPEN  INPUT  TRANSFILE
    STOCKMAST
   OUTPUT NEW-STOCKMAS
    QUERYFILE
    PRINT-FILE
  PERFORM READ-TRANSFILE
  PERFORM READ-STOCKMAST.
  PERFORM NEW-HEADINGS.

  UPDATE-PROCESS.
   EVALUATE TRUE
    WHEN PART-NUM < M-PART-NUM
     PERFORM PRINT-DETAIL
     PERFORM READ-TRANSFILE
    WHEN PART-NUM > M-PART-NUM
            WRITE N-STOCK-REC FROM STOCKM-REC
     PERFORM READ-STOCKMAST
    WHEN PART-NUM = M-PART-NUM
     PERFORM CALCULATE-DATA
     PERFORM READ-TRANSFILE
     END-EVALUATE.

  CALCULATE-DATA.
   MOVE TRANS-DATE TO L-TRANS-DATE
   IF TRANS-TYPE = "R" THEN
    COMPUTE BALANCE = BALANCE + QUANTITY
   ELSE
    COMPUTE BALANCE = BALANCE - QUANTITY
   END-IF
   IF BALANCE < MIN OR BALANCE > MAX THEN
    WRITE QUERY-REC FROM STOCKM-REC
   END-IF.

  READ-TRANSFILE.
   READ TRANSFILE AT END
    MOVE HIGH-VALUES TO PART-NUM
   END-READ.

  READ-STOCKMAST.
   READ STOCKMAST AT END
    MOVE HIGH-VALUES TO M-PART-NUM
   END-READ.

  NEW-HEADINGS.
          WRITE PRINT-REC FROM W-MAIN-HEAD
   WRITE PRINT-REC FROM W-SUB-HEAD AFTER 3.

  PRINT-DETAIL.
   MOVE TRANS-TYPE TO P-TRANS-TYPE
   MOVE PART-NUM TO P-PART-NUM
   MOVE C-S-NUM TO P-C-S-NUM
   MOVE TRANS-DATE TO P-TRANS-DATE
   MOVE QUANTITY TO P-QUANTITY
   WRITE PRINT-REC FROM W-DETAIL AFTER 1
    AT END-OF-PAGE
     PERFORM NEW-HEADINGS
   END-WRITE.

  FINAL-PROCESS.
   CLOSE TRANSFILE
    STOCKMAST
    NEW-STOCKMAS
    QUERYFILE
    PRINT-FILE.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: help required

_(thread depth: 6)_

- **From:** dxmixxer@netdirect.net (Doug Miller)

In article <SOga3.1041$BS6.491@wards>,
   "Stuart Ward" <stuart@printech.force9.co.uk> wrote:
+Heres the data I used and I can't fault it using this.  The question clearly
+states that all data files are previousley validated and the master stock
+file cannot contain two entries for the same part number. If you still say
+that theres a problem could you elaberate on the data req to reproduce it.

I didn't say that you needed two records in the master with the same part
number -- I said "the first two transactions having the same part number
as the first two records in the master file." For example:

Master		Transaction
-------		-----------
00000001	00000001
00000002	00000002
..		...

Your code will read the first record from each file and correctly update
the master record for 00000001 with the transaction data. It will then
incorrectly read *only* the next record from the transaction file (part
number 00000002) and compare it to the master record (part number 00000001).
What happens next?


+
+Doug Miller <dxmixxer@netdirect.net> wrote in message
+news:7k6vdk$s8_002@news.netdirect.net...
+> In article <7Uo93.3854$Hy4.3374@wards>,
+>    "Stuart Ward" <stuart@printech.force9.co.uk> wrote:

+> +
+> +TRANSFILE
+> +    WHEN PART-NUM > M-PART-NUM
+> +            WRITE N-STOCK-REC FROM STOCKM-REC
+> +     PERFORM READ-STOCKMAST
+>
+> No it isn't.
+>
+> Try a test file with the first two transactions having the same part
+number as
+> the first two records in the master file.
+>
+> --
+> dlmiller/at/netdirect/dot/net
+
+
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: help required

_(thread depth: 11)_

- **From:** "Stuart Ward" <stuart@printech.force9.co.uk>
- **Date:** 1999-06-21T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<ohob3.3586$w61.1345@stones>`
- **References:** `<XlQ83.3437$Hy4.2877@wards> <3763e3fe@news3.us.ibm.net> <yNR83.497$S2.33693@iad-read.news.verio.net> <vfU83.3480$Hy4.3040@wards> <7k1rhp$168_003@news.netdirect.net> <7Uo93.3854$Hy4.3374@wards> <7k6vdk$s8_002@news.netdirect.net> <SOga3.1041$BS6.491@wards> <7keppd$3bc_001@news.netdirect.net> <_VWa3.3110$w61.801@stones> <hgob3.3585$w61.1326@stones>`

```
 Repost of source code in case you've lost it


IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
 PROGRAM-ID. QUESTION-B.

 ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
 INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION.
 FILE-CONTROL.
  SELECT TRANSFILE ASSIGN TO "TRANS.DAT"
   ORGANIZATION LINE SEQUENTIAL.
  SELECT STOCKMAST ASSIGN TO "STOCKMA.DAT"
   ORGANIZATION LINE SEQUENTIAL.
  SELECT NEW-STOCKMAS ASSIGN TO "NSTOCKMA.DAT"
   ORGANIZATION LINE SEQUENTIAL.
  SELECT QUERYFILE ASSIGN TO "QUERY.DAT"
   ORGANIZATION LINE SEQUENTIAL.
  SELECT PRINT-FILE ASSIGN TO PRINTER.

 DATA DIVISION.
 FILE SECTION.
 FD TRANSFILE.
 01 TRANS-REC.
         03 PART-NUM  PIC X(8).
   88 END-OF-TRANS VALUE HIGH-VALUES.
  03 TRANS-TYPE  PIC X.
         03 C-S-NUM   PIC 9(6).
  03 TRANS-DATE  PIC 9(6).
  03 QUANTITY  PIC 9(6).

 FD STOCKMAST.
 01 STOCKM-REC.
  03 M-PART-NUM  PIC X(8).
   88 END-OF-MAST VALUE HIGH-VALUES.
  03 L-TRANS-DATE  PIC 9(6).
  03 BALANCE   PIC S9(6).
  03 MIN   PIC 9(6).
  03 MAX   PIC 9(6).

 FD QUERYFILE.
 01 QUERY-REC   PIC X(33).

 FD NEW-STOCKMAS.
 01 N-STOCK-REC   PIC X(33).

 FD PRINT-FILE
  LINAGE IS 60 LINES
  WITH FOOTING AT 56
  LINES AT TOP 2
  LINES AT BOTTOM 4.
 01 PRINT-REC   PIC X(71).

 WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.


 01 W-MAIN-HEAD.
         03    PIC X(34) VALUE SPACES.
         03    PIC X(12) VALUE
                 "ERROR REPORT".

 01 W-SUB-HEAD.
  03    PIC X(5) VALUE SPACES.
  03    PIC X(12) VALUE
   "PART NO.".
  03    PIC X(13) VALUE
   "TRANS TYPE".
  03    PIC X(17) VALUE
   "CUST/SUPP NO.".
  03    PIC X(14) VALUE
   "TRANS DATE".
  03    PIC X(8) VALUE
   "QUANTITY".

 01 W-DETAIL.
  03    PIC X(5) VALUE SPACES.
  03 P-PART-NUM  PIC X(8).
  03    PIC X(8) VALUE SPACES.
  03 P-TRANS-TYPE  PIC X.
  03    PIC X(11) VALUE SPACES.
  03 P-C-S-NUM  PIC X(6).
  03    PIC X(10) VALUE SPACES.
  03 P-TRANS-DATE  PIC X(6).
  03    PIC X(7) VALUE SPACES.
  03 P-QUANTITY  PIC Z(6)9.

 PROCEDURE DIVISION.
 MAIN-CONTROL.
  PERFORM INITIAL-PROCESS
  PERFORM UPDATE-PROCESS UNTIL
   END-OF-TRANS AND END-OF-MAST
  PERFORM FINAL-PROCESS
  STOP RUN.

 INITIAL-PROCESS.
  OPEN  INPUT  TRANSFILE
    STOCKMAST
   OUTPUT NEW-STOCKMAS
    QUERYFILE
    PRINT-FILE
  PERFORM READ-TRANSFILE
  PERFORM READ-STOCKMAST.
  PERFORM NEW-HEADINGS.

  UPDATE-PROCESS.
   EVALUATE TRUE
    WHEN PART-NUM < M-PART-NUM
     PERFORM PRINT-DETAIL
     PERFORM READ-TRANSFILE
    WHEN PART-NUM > M-PART-NUM
            WRITE N-STOCK-REC FROM STOCKM-REC
     PERFORM READ-STOCKMAST
    WHEN PART-NUM = M-PART-NUM
     PERFORM CALCULATE-DATA
     PERFORM READ-TRANSFILE
     END-EVALUATE.

  CALCULATE-DATA.
   MOVE TRANS-DATE TO L-TRANS-DATE
   IF TRANS-TYPE = "R" THEN
    COMPUTE BALANCE = BALANCE + QUANTITY
   ELSE
    COMPUTE BALANCE = BALANCE - QUANTITY
   END-IF
   IF BALANCE < MIN OR BALANCE > MAX THEN
    WRITE QUERY-REC FROM STOCKM-REC
   END-IF.

  READ-TRANSFILE.
   READ TRANSFILE AT END
    MOVE HIGH-VALUES TO PART-NUM
   END-READ.

  READ-STOCKMAST.
   READ STOCKMAST AT END
    MOVE HIGH-VALUES TO M-PART-NUM
   END-READ.

  NEW-HEADINGS.
          WRITE PRINT-REC FROM W-MAIN-HEAD
   WRITE PRINT-REC FROM W-SUB-HEAD AFTER 3.

  PRINT-DETAIL.
   MOVE TRANS-TYPE TO P-TRANS-TYPE
   MOVE PART-NUM TO P-PART-NUM
   MOVE C-S-NUM TO P-C-S-NUM
   MOVE TRANS-DATE TO P-TRANS-DATE
   MOVE QUANTITY TO P-QUANTITY
   WRITE PRINT-REC FROM W-DETAIL AFTER 1
    AT END-OF-PAGE
     PERFORM NEW-HEADINGS
   END-WRITE.

  FINAL-PROCESS.
   CLOSE TRANSFILE
    STOCKMAST
    NEW-STOCKMAS
    QUERYFILE
    PRINT-FILE
```

```
I can't recall the original question!!!

However, be aware that "Signed" numeric fields created by one compiler
(ie MicroFocus) might not have the same value for the overpunch (sign 
nibble) as others.  If you are using Fujitsu this is particularly true
- I found that the "last character" of a signed numeric created by 
Realia was different than that created by Fujitsu.



On Fri, 18 Jun 1999 10:54:56, "Stuart Ward" 
<stuart@printech.force9.co.uk> wrote:

> 
> > Heres the data I used and I can't fault it using this.  The question
…[53 more quoted lines elided]…
> 

-------------------------
Trust the computer industry to shorten "Year 2000" to Y2K.  It was 
this
kind of thinking that caused the problem in the first place.

Try a better search engine: http://www.google.com

Visit my updated website at
http://www.geocities.com/Eureka/2006/
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: De-compiler for RM Cobol 85

- **From:** Barticus@att.spam.net (Randall Bart)
- **Date:** 1999-06-15T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** alt.cobol,comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3765a3d4.8048489@netnews>`
- **References:** `<01beb5d4$32f58d00$cfcfa8c0@murphy> <ZYU83.78$Ea2.349306942@dca1-nnrp1.news.digex.net> <37642566@news3.us.ibm.net>`

```
'Twas Sun, 13 Jun 1999 16:40:58 -0500, when "Leif Svalgaard"
<lsvalg@ibm.net> illuminated comp.lang.cobol thusly:

> Howard,
> That is news to me!
> What form is the de-compiled code in?
> Tokens or readable Cobol?

It varies with the compiler and the de-compiler.  If the compiler saves
symbolic debugging info, you even get your original datanames back.  In
most cases you get code with generated names, and many cases of GO TO
where you had ELSE or vice versa.  If the compiler does optimization, the
decompiled code may look quite convoluted.
```

#### ↳ Re: De-compiler for RM Cobol 85

- **From:** "Warren Porter" <warrenp123@netdoor123.com>
- **Date:** 1999-06-14T00:00:00

#### ↳ Re: De-compiler for RM Cobol 85

- **From:** Nige <znige@zwg.zicl.co.uk>
- **Date:** 1999-06-14T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** alt.cobol,comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3764E54B.413A6F54@zwg.zicl.co.uk>`
- **References:** `<01beb5d4$32f58d00$cfcfa8c0@murphy>`

```
hostmaster wrote:
> 
> We've "lost" a crucial bit of source code . . . .
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
> [btw: I'm not a programmer - I'm just the business manager ;-)]

It's sometimes possible to have a good go a hand decompiling, especially
if it's been compiled with diagnostics in (which will give you the
identifier names and line numbers).  Depends a lot on the compiler and
machine architecture.

BUT... why do you want to do this?  Would patching the existing compiled
module be more appropriate?  Or commissioning someone to rewrite it from 
scratch - you'd probably end up with something better than the original
this way.

     Cheers,
        Nige
```

<a id="t-4ac8d46738"></a>
## Thread: Newbie: Help w/ WRITE, REWRITE and DELETE statements. — 13 msgs

_Topics: [`Migration and conversion`](topics.md#migration) · [`Tutorials, books, learning`](topics.md#learning)_

### Newbie: Help w/ WRITE, REWRITE and DELETE statements.

- **From:** Carlos Featherston <featherston@intranet.ca>
- **Date:** 1999-06-13T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<376406AE.DFA342C5@intranet.ca>`

```
Hi all,

I am working on a school project that requires me to update
(non-sequentailly) a master file from information contained in a
transaction file. Easy enough, correct? This is COBOL 101, correct?
Correct! Why then, can't I make the following piece of code work? I know
there is a problem in how I am writing the WRITE, REWRITE and DELETE
statements, but for the life of me I can't figure where.

151-ADD-RECORD.
           WRITE MASTER-RECORD FROM WS-MASTER-RECORD
               INVALID KEY
                   MOVE TR-INFO TO WS-STOCK-INFO
                   MOVE TR-CURRENT-INFO TO WS-STOCK-CURRENT-INFO
                   MOVE TR-PROJECTION-INFO TO WS-STOCK-PROJECTION-INFO
               NOT INVALID KEY
                   MOVE TR-NAME TO RPT-STOCK-NAME
                   MOVE 'Error - A - Duplicate Record Found'
                       TO RPT-ERROR-REASON
                   WRITE REPORT-RECORD FROM REPORT-RECORD-DET
                       AFTER ADVANCING 1 LINE
           END-WRITE.

I am just posting the piece of code that deals with adding a record to
the master file, although those for correcting and deleting records have
the same format (where applicable) as the one above. Essentially all 3
statements, as I have been taught are as follows:

        WRITE | REWRITE | DELETE record-name | record-name | file-name
                INVALID KEY imperative-statement-1
                NOT INVALID KEY imperative-statement-2
        END-WRITE | REWRITE | DELETE

Well, isn't what I wrote the same as what I was taught? Should I be also
looking elsewhere in the code to solve this problem? I have accomplished
the same project using switches for the master file and a variety of IF
statements to supplant the above dreaded statements and the program
works. PLEASE HELP!

Thanks in advance to all...Carlos
```

#### ↳ Re: Newbie: Help w/ WRITE, REWRITE and DELETE statements.

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 1999-06-13T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7k12mk$7dv@dfw-ixnews5.ix.netcom.com>`
- **References:** `<376406AE.DFA342C5@intranet.ca>`

```
It would help us to help you if you told us what makes you think that the
program is NOT working.  Is it getting a compile error?  A run-time error?
Just not creating the file the way you think it should?

Without that information, it is just a "shot in the dark" - but my guess is
that you have not put the correct ACCESS phrase in your Select/Assign
statement - that support "Write" with the "invalid key" phrase.
```

```
Hi Bill,

Sorry, I should have included that info as well. It is not a compilation or
run-time error, but rather an error in my logic I suspect, since the program
compiles and runs cleanly. The program also calls for non-matching and
duplicate records to be rejected and and a rejection event to be logged in a
report for further evaluation. I have checked that ACCESS MODE IS RANDOM is in
my code, and it is. However, as someone else in this group pointed out, it
might be a matter of having forgotten an END-WRITE in the most unsuspecting of
places. In the meantime, I'll make the additions to my code as suggested and
see what happens. Nevertheless, any help is still welcomed. Thanks
again...Carlos

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Newbie: Help w/ WRITE, REWRITE and DELETE statements.

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 1999-06-13T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7k190t$334@sjx-ixn9.ix.netcom.com>`
- **References:** `<376406AE.DFA342C5@intranet.ca> <7k12mk$7dv@dfw-ixnews5.ix.netcom.com> <37641D84.CCDC5B6C@intranet.ca>`

```
Am I correct that the symptom that you are seeing is that records that you
would expect to be in the "REPORT-RECORD-DET" file are not there?

Are there other records in this file, but just not the "duplicate record"
ones?

Have you verified that "RPT-STOCK-NAME" and "RPT-ERROR-REASON" are in the
working-storage layout that you are doing your WRITE FROM?

Finally, you might want to add a "DISPLAY your-file-status-value" statement
after the WRITE FROM to verify that the record is actually getting written -
and that you aren't getting some I/O error during the write.  (If you do get
an error, then check out WHY that is happening.)
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Newbie: Help w/ WRITE, REWRITE and DELETE statements.

- **From:** "Rick Smith" <ricksmith@aiservices.com>
- **Date:** 1999-06-15T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7k5iqb$rnb$1@server.cntfl.com>`
- **References:** `<376406AE.DFA342C5@intranet.ca> <7k12q8$q1c$1@server.cntfl.com> <3764219A.528ADCE1@intranet.ca>`

```
[Repost due to news server problems.]
Carlos Featherston wrote in message <3764219A.528ADCE1@intranet.ca>...
>Thanks for your help Rick, but suggestion has not worked. Maybe I am not
>expressing myself properly. The only reason I suspect it is an error in my
…[7 more quoted lines elided]…
>problem. Thanks again...Carlos.

I understand you to be saying that your only indication that
transactions have been processed is the error report.

You stated, elsewhere, that you are using Micro Focus 16-bit.
Have you tried animating the program?

I will assume that you have an EVALUATE to select among
Add, Update, and Delete routines. How do you know that
you are actually performing any of these routines? Could
the logic problem be that the EVALUATE selects nothing?

Assuming this does not solve the problem. It might help,
if we could see the loop that reads and processes the
transactions.
------------------
Rick Smith

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Newbie: Help w/ WRITE, REWRITE and DELETE statements.

- **From:** dxmixxer@netdirect.net (Doug Miller)
- **Date:** 1999-06-14T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7k1rac$168_002@news.netdirect.net>`
- **References:** `<376406AE.DFA342C5@intranet.ca> <7k12q8$q1c$1@server.cntfl.com>`

```
In article <7k12q8$q1c$1@server.cntfl.com>,
   "Rick Smith" <ricksmith@aiservices.com> wrote:
+
+Carlos Featherston wrote in message <376406AE.DFA342C5@intranet.ca>...
+[...]
+>151-ADD-RECORD.
+>           WRITE MASTER-RECORD FROM WS-MASTER-RECORD
+>               INVALID KEY
+[...]
+>               NOT INVALID KEY
+[...]
+>                   WRITE REPORT-RECORD FROM REPORT-RECORD-DET
+>                       AFTER ADVANCING 1 LINE
+add here     end-write
+>           END-WRITE.
+
+Add another END-WRITE at the position noted. That should
+take care of it.

If if does, his compiler is broken. The period following END-WRITE terminates 
the scope of all delimited-scope statements in the sentence; thus, 
		END-WRITE
	END-WRITE
and
	END-WRITE.
will have the same effect here.
```

#### ↳ Re: Newbie: Help w/ WRITE, REWRITE and DELETE statements.

- **From:** redsky@ibm.net (Thane Hubbell)
- **Date:** 1999-06-14T00:00:00

```
On Sun, 13 Jun 1999 19:22:27 GMT, Carlos Featherston
<featherston@intranet.ca> wrote:

I've seen some compilers that have problems with nested WRITE
statements.  (ie Writes within the imperatives).  I changed these to
PERFORM these writes and the problem went away.  As I recall
MicroFocus 3.4 exhibited this problem.  

>Correct! Why then, can't I make the following piece of code work? I know
>there is a problem in how I am writing the WRITE, REWRITE and DELETE
…[7 more quoted lines elided]…
>                   MOVE TR-PROJECTION-INFO TO WS-STOCK-PROJECTION-INFO

???????? now what?

>               NOT INVALID KEY
>                   MOVE TR-NAME TO RPT-STOCK-NAME
>                   MOVE 'Error - A - Duplicate Record Found'
>                       TO RPT-ERROR-REASON

???????? perform the WRITE instead of doing it here.

>                   WRITE REPORT-RECORD FROM REPORT-RECORD-DET
>                       AFTER ADVANCING 1 LINE
>           END-WRITE.
>
```

#### ↳ Re: Newbie: Help w/ WRITE, REWRITE and DELETE statements.

- **From:** dxmixxer@netdirect.net (Doug Miller)
- **Date:** 1999-06-14T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7k1r53$168_001@news.netdirect.net>`
- **References:** `<376406AE.DFA342C5@intranet.ca>`

```
In article <376406AE.DFA342C5@intranet.ca>,
   Carlos Featherston <featherston@intranet.ca> wrote:
+Hi all,
+
+I am working on a school project that requires me to update
+(non-sequentailly) a master file from information contained in a
+transaction file. Easy enough, correct? This is COBOL 101, correct?
+Correct! Why then, can't I make the following piece of code work? I know
+there is a problem in how I am writing the WRITE, REWRITE and DELETE
+statements, but for the life of me I can't figure where.

One of us is missing something horribly obvious, and I think it might
be you. :-) Are you *sure* that your "INVALID KEY" and "NOT INVALID KEY" 
clauses are correct? At first glance, they appear *reversed* to me:
+
+151-ADD-RECORD.
+           WRITE MASTER-RECORD FROM WS-MASTER-RECORD
+               INVALID KEY
This clause is executed if the write *fails* -- but I think you want
to do these moves if it *succeeds*.
+                   MOVE TR-INFO TO WS-STOCK-INFO
+                   MOVE TR-CURRENT-INFO TO WS-STOCK-CURRENT-INFO
+                   MOVE TR-PROJECTION-INFO TO WS-STOCK-PROJECTION-INFO
+               NOT INVALID KEY
And this clause is executed if the write *succeeds* -- and I'm sure that you 
don't want to be displaying a duplicate-record error message after a 
successful write.
+                   MOVE TR-NAME TO RPT-STOCK-NAME
+                   MOVE 'Error - A - Duplicate Record Found'
+                       TO RPT-ERROR-REASON
+                   WRITE REPORT-RECORD FROM REPORT-RECORD-DET
+                       AFTER ADVANCING 1 LINE
+           END-WRITE.

I think if you change "INVALID KEY" to "NOT INVALID KEY", and vice versa,
the program will behave as you expect it to.
```

<a id="t-60287fb0b2"></a>
## Thread: COBOL Standards — 3 msgs

_Topics: [`COBOL standards (ANS, ISO, 74/85/2002/2014)`](topics.md#standards)_

### COBOL Standards

- **From:** "James J. Gavan" <jjgavan@home.com>
- **Date:** 1999-06-13T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<376347E8.2A712CC3@home.com>`

```
As this is a major topic I have started a new thread.

In response to the topic "I need a decent COBOL compiler" Bill Klein
wrote :-

> As the major proponent of "killing off" (or more accurately radically
> modifying) the draft's "processor dependent" definition, I certainly
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
> doesn't destroy this - but it does "wound" it quite a bit.

>I should (just so you understand why I SOMETIMES disagree with what I >am saying here), say that I think that the next Standard *must* >interact as well as possible with C, C++, and all those operating >system APIs that have become so popular.  To do this, I think that >COBOL *must* add some non-portable stuff - just to interact  
>successfully with other languages and functions that aren't portable.

Bill, 

I've just recently got more 'web-involved' and started looking at
comp.lang.cobol. I'm delighted you mentioned Standards "You don't hear
from us - the developers". From a cursory search I did some months ago I
saw a "Standards" site which said contact your representative - who do I
contact here in Canada, Nanook of the North !

COBOL evolved initially because IBM, Unisys et al were trying to sell
the Pentagon on their specific machine "architectures" with their own 
machine code and assemblers. Somebody had the guts to say "Enough
already!" - we'll formalize COBOL - let your machine interpret.

As an end-user pointed out to me, "COBOL is free". He was referring to
the standard intro you see in books "COBOL is an industry language and
is not the property etc.....". It's the toolkits we pay for. 

As you know the COBOL OO Standards have already 'tipped their hat' to
the work done on Smalltalk, and in terms of COBOL standards, I believe
the following are relevant quotes :-

"He,  (Peter Deutsch - one of the many SmallTalk designers), strongly
opposed  integration with native user interface toolkits on the grounds
that it would untowardly increase the maintenance burden from the
platform fan out (that is, the need for different implementations for
different operating system/user interface combinations). He advocated
emulating the platform in Smalltalk using a framework approach, and then
later endorsed using a framework whose concrete subclasses would link to
the platform's toolkit. Allowing programs to invoke platform toolkit
capabilities directly, he correctly predicted, would result in
destruction of real portability. (Adele Goldberg - OO Languages 1998).".

"The tools industry is abuzz with the virtues of Java, but most of the
features present in Java have been part of Smalltalk for more than 25
years. .......... Because the tools manufacturers make money only on the
sale of development tools, training and upgrades, the stagnation of the
C++ market to date has launched the Java market into the forefront....."
(Victoria & Jonathan Pletzke - OO Languages 1998) - So much for the hype
about Java !

Within so-called Standard COBOL we have variations from compiler to
compiler. To insist that the standard should be "concrete" would inhibit
new ideas - but it would help considerably if there was an agreed
standard on representation of data in standard cobol files, obviating
the necessity of toolkit converters when moving data from MicroFocus to
Fujitsu or from Ryan-McFarland to Micro Focus etc.

Perhaps my biggest concern with the "Standards" is that although the
committees will come up with recommendations, will Base and GUI classes
for Object Orientation differ dramatically from one compiler to the
next ? I rather suspect that they will. If I create a collection or
dictionary in Fujitsu or Liant(RM) will it need a rewrite for
Merant(MicroFocus) NetExpress ?

Such an eventuality would mean that each compiler would contain compiler
specific toolkits for OO classes - and the word COBOL starts to become
meaningless.

In the same context that a standard was laid down for the original COBOL
68, have the current Standards Committees considered the possibility of
a COBOL-specific virtual machine with the flexibility to recognize
different operating systems. (Here I'm thinking on the "openness" of
Linux and the ability of any computer geek to write new code). Open
toolkits - not a very attractive idea to compiler developers !

Jimmy, Calgary AB
```

#### ↳ Re: COBOL Standards

- **From:** "Warren Simmons" <w.g.simmons@worldnet.att.net>
- **Date:** 1999-06-13T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7k1j0n$8p6$1@bgtnsc02.worldnet.att.net>`
- **References:** `<376347E8.2A712CC3@home.com>`

```
Great thinking, James..... it would seem that the need to have processor
dependent language material in the standard
could be replaced by the need to interface the standard instead.  Not only
that, but the effort might be more in the spirit of the COBOL objectives.
As far as the impact on compiler writers is concerned, that is the name of
the game. Provide the tool as needed or find another market.

Warren Simmons

James J. Gavan <jjgavan@home.com> wrote in message
news:376347E8.2A712CC3@home.com...
> As this is a major topic I have started a new thread.
>
…[11 more quoted lines elided]…
> >I should (just so you understand why I SOMETIMES disagree with what I >am
saying here), say that I think that the next Standard *must* >interact as
well as possible with C, C++, and all those operating >system APIs that have
become so popular.  To do this, I think that >COBOL *must* add some
non-portable stuff - just to interact
> >successfully with other languages and functions that aren't portable.
>
…[65 more quoted lines elided]…
> Jimmy, Calgary AB
```

For anyone outside the US, if you are interested in contacting your national
body, a web page that you can start at is the WG4 (COBOL working group for
COBOL) - which is at:

 http://anubis.dkuug.dk/jtc1/sc22/wg4/

(check out the links to "your" and  "national body")

The person who has in the past represented Canada at WG4 doesn't have an
email address listed.  However, on the J4 mailing list, he is listed as:

   Ian Mooney (F) (D)
  Computer Horizons Canada Corp.
  66 Scarsdale Rd
  Toronto, M3B 2R7
  Canada
  Tel:  (416) 391-3600  ext 6010
  Fax:  (416) 385-7144

I do NOT know if he is still involved with Canada's representation in COBOL
Standards development, but it would be my guess that he could set you on the
correct path.
```

- **From:** "Nuno Sarandes" <nmls@esoterica.pt>

- **Message-ID:** `<929240181.564770@gorgoroth.esoterica.pt>`
- **References:** `<7jdn3h$3s8$1@nclient3-gui.server.virgin.net>`

```
Basically, you must fill the record key with the identification of the
record you want and execute a start and a read. If the status is 00, then
the record was found. If it's 23, the record was not found.

---

Al Will's posting is so polite and progessional that folks should apply just to
compliment him for being so well behaved and so unusual in this newsgroup.
Realy, it's a fine ad.

But I can not just let this go by. Do you see that this ad says that endevor is
such a piece of junk, and that it is so junky and utterly junkitude that they
are going to pay
folks $70.00 / hour to move your code into production?!  If this were not
Friday night, I might think it is the ad that is making me laugh this hard.

When you need a PhD to move somehting up, then it is time to go back to the
drawing board.  Is this ad really posted in the same newsgroup as the utterly
serious cross chatter about the virtuousness of column 73-80 for maintenance
annotation?

Why can't they just let us program?

The only thing that is more fun is column 1-6 for maintenance annotation
practice that prevents auto renumber facilities and compels NONUMB dataset
attributes.

As gargantuum as endevor is, I frequently encounter shops where under emergency
conditions you discover there is no current source listing (No verb list, no
map: nothin' ! You have to hope the current production release parms are the
ones that were used to gen the program you are cogitatin' and kind of deploy a
sampling sort of thing to support debuging.) - it is just this kind of
circumstance that makes COBOLers into Assembler afficionadoes. 

But you know, that is not how this is supposed to go! Software maintenance
practices are supposed to make the technicians job easier. 

If the librarians in St. Paul are getting paid $70.00/hr then they are getting
more than a large portion of the programmers there.  I honestly believe that
the ad clearly indicates that endevor is not the solution we have all been
looking for.

Best Wishes, 

Robert Rayhawk
RKRayhawk@aol.com
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ MVS: SCLM discussion - PC / UNIX guru's ignore.

_(thread depth: 4)_

- **From:** Ken Foskey <waratah@zip.com.au>
- **Date:** 1999-06-21T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<376E29AE.42CCCDC0@zip.com.au>`
- **References:** `<alwillFDBvEw.428@netcom.com> <19990618205855.10714.00000562@ng-fy1.aol.com> <376BAB52.5216AC4B@zip.com.au> <e1B81Zru#GA.281@cpmsnbbsa03>`

```
Robert M. Pritchett - RMP Consulting Partners LLC wrote:
> 
> I've wondered about SCLM, glad to hear it's good. I've used LMF a few
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
> 

SCLM is LMF extended.

SCLM requires the following library structure:

	PROJECT.LEVEL.TYPE

Where project is the division of the application PAYROLL or
CLIENT1,
LEVEL is the position in life cycle eg DEV, TEST, UAT, PROD,  TYPE
is the content eg JCL, SOURCE, MACRO.

The packaging (grouping of programs within a change) is called an
ARCHDEF. For example it may contain program1, program2, program3.

When you develop a module containing many subroutines you define
the program in an archdef such as:

   INLCD  PROGRAM1  SOURCE
   INCLD  MODULE1   SOURCE
   INCLD  MODULE2   SOURCE

   LOAD   PROGRAM1

It automatically compiles the modules that have changed and links
them in.  In the same way as Unix Make works only the changed
modules are compiled.

If you wish to change a copybook you never have to worry, create
an archdef including all the programs containing the copybook and
then build (compile) it.  Once you have done this you promote the
change through the environments.

if you change a single unit within a more complex change (say UAT
finds an error).  You go into edit the module is automatically
copied from UAT into the lowest level, you make your changes and
then build and promote the main archdef.  If you only changed one
module then that is all that is copied up.

The copy is also interesting:  The compile is only done at the
bottom and then the load module is copied between groups.  If
something goes out of step (eg you change a copybook and forget to
build or compile all the rest of the programs) the promotion will
fail.  This ensures that the programs you tested are what actually
goes live.

This is a great system that comes for free with ISPF.  It really
is a pain to set up but once you have and are used to it there is
nothing easier to run.

Ken
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: SCLM discussion - PC / UNIX guru's ignore.

_(thread depth: 5)_

- **From:** "Robert M. Pritchett - RMP Consulting Partners LLC" <MSN.Bus.News@rmpcp.com>

have something to do that?

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: SCLM discussion - PC / UNIX guru's ignore.

_(thread depth: 6)_

- **From:** Ken Foskey <waratah@zip.com.au>
- **Date:** 1999-06-26T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3774BF92.DFFF299A@zip.com.au>`
- **References:** `<alwillFDBvEw.428@netcom.com> <19990618205855.10714.00000562@ng-fy1.aol.com> <376BAB52.5216AC4B@zip.com.au> <e1B81Zru#GA.281@cpmsnbbsa03> <376E29AE.42CCCDC0@zip.com.au> <evKRXF4v#GA.309@cpmsnbbsa03>`

```
Robert M. Pritchett - RMP Consulting Partners LLC wrote:
> 
> Sounds neat except do you really have to define/list everything
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
> 

When you first set it up (on historical programs) then you have a
utility that scans the loadlib and generates an include deck for
you.

When you are defining a program for the first time (or adding a
subroutine) then it really is not that difficult.  It is very
explicit about what is imbedded by design.  It really is not as
difficult as it may sound.

Ken
```

## Thread: A bit of career advice, please. — 8 msgs

_Topics: [`Jobs, careers, recruiting, salary`](topics.md#jobs)_

### A bit of career advice, please.

- **From:** "Joseph Finkeldey" <joefink1@MUNGEmindspring.com>
- **Date:** 1999-06-14T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7k359t$ajl$1@nntp9.atl.mindspring.net>`

```
I am currently going to school for programming at Chubb Institute,  (very
heavy on COBOL), and have heard from many people that it's difficult to get
your first job, since experience counts for so much.

My tutor tells me she knows people who have not been able to get that first
job after 5 months of searching!!

Please tell me if there is anything I can do to increase my chances of
getting a job quicker, or if this is even worth it -- versus network or tech
support?

Thanks, Joe.

(remove MUNGE from my email address)
```

#### ↳ Re: A bit of career advice, please.

- **From:** "Warren Porter" <warrenp123@netdoor123.com>
- **Date:** 1999-06-14T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<Era93.1885$LI5.184005@axe.netdoor.com>`
- **References:** `<7k359t$ajl$1@nntp9.atl.mindspring.net>`

```
No computer shop exists for its own sake;  the "Computer Services" section
exists to DO SOMETHING for the rest of the business.  It is very much in
your interest to know what that SOMETHING is.  If you already know (or at
least have minored in) accounting, bookkeeping, or business you will be
ahead of anybody who will have to be told what a journal or a ledger is
before starting.

Bringing in some samples of your work showing your grasp of COBOL and the
structure of your code (identifiable, maintainable by anyone) would also
help.

Joseph Finkeldey wrote in message <7k359t$ajl$1@nntp9.atl.mindspring.net>...
>I am currently going to school for programming at Chubb Institute,  (very
>heavy on COBOL), and have heard from many people that it's difficult to get
…[6 more quoted lines elided]…
>getting a job quicker, or if this is even worth it -- versus network or
tech
>support?
```

<a id="t-79455e59e0"></a>
## Thread: IBM COBOL calling Assembly subroutine — 6 msgs

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers)_

### IBM COBOL calling Assembly subroutine

- **From:** "News" <goobsa@hotmail.com>
- **Date:** 1999-06-14T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7k3047$qo$1@clematis.singnet.com.sg>`

```
I had an existing assembler routine which worked fine with os/vs cobol.
When I call the same routine in IBM COBOL, it gives some trouble.[link-edit
error]

IEW2278I B352 INVOCATION PARAMETERS -
LET,LIST,MAP,XREF,SIZE=(384K,96K),RMODE=ANY,AMODE=31
IEW2635I 4B34 THREE BYTE ADCON IN SECTION CDATE AT OFFSET 00002851 IN CLASS
B_TEXT WITH RMODE=ANY CANNOT BE RELOCATED.

When I begin my assembly routine like following, the above error solved.
But, I get cobol runtime error. RC=4000
CEE3250C The system or user abend S0C4 ...

CDATE     CSECT
CDATE     RMODE ANY
CDATE     AMODE 31
               STM 14,12,12(13)
                ......
                ......

Any help will be appreciated.
TIA.
```

#### ↳ Re: IBM COBOL calling Assembly subroutine

- **From:** scomstock@aol.com (S Comstock)
- **Date:** 1999-06-14T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<19990614094123.04794.00001477@ng-fs1.aol.com>`
- **References:** `<7k3047$qo$1@clematis.singnet.com.sg>`

```
The I-would-like-help-but-I-don't-want-to-tell-you-who-I-am -named "News"
<goobsa@hotmail.com> writes ...
>I had an existing assembler routine which worked fine with os/vs cobol.
>When I call the same routine in IBM COBOL, it gives some trouble.[link-edit
…[19 more quoted lines elided]…
>TIA.

So, we need to see more of the Assembler code. You have requested AMODE31 and
RMODE ANY but apparently you have a three byte adcon in your program (check for
a DCB); Three byte adcon's can only address up to the line, not above it. So
they need to be physically located below the line.

Try changing your RMODE to be RMODE 24. If this doesn't work, post more of your
code so we can look at it.

Regards,


Steve Comstock
Telephone: 303-393-8716
www.trainersfriend.com
email: steve@trainersfriend.com
256-B S. Monaco Parkway
Denver, CO 80224
USA
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: IBM COBOL calling Assembly subroutine

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 1999-06-14T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7k3qcu$knc@dfw-ixnews12.ix.netcom.com>`
- **References:** `<7k3047$qo$1@clematis.singnet.com.sg> <19990614094123.04794.00001477@ng-fs1.aol.com>`

```
In case you are not EXPLICITLY asking for this to be RMODE=ANY and AMODE=31,
check out your compiler options for RENT and RES (if you are using VS COBOL
II) or RMODE (if you are using IBM COBOL for this-and-that).  They may be
asking for AMODE/RMODEs that your called assembler won't support.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: IBM COBOL calling Assembly subroutine

- **From:** "Goobsa C" <goobsa@hotmail.com>
- **Date:** 1999-06-16T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7k6dr4$i3g$1@clematis.singnet.com.sg>`
- **References:** `<7k3047$qo$1@clematis.singnet.com.sg> <19990614094123.04794.00001477@ng-fs1.aol.com> <7k3qcu$knc@dfw-ixnews12.ix.netcom.com>`

```
Thanks for your suggestions.

There was prob in DCB. When I changed RMODE to 24 in the assembly code
as well as in COBOL compilation jcl, there is no run-time error.

We are using HIGH LEVEL ASSEMBLER, 5696-234, RELEASE 2.0;
OS/390 V2R4 and IBM COBOL.

Regards.
Goobsa C
_______________
<snip>
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: IBM COBOL calling Assembly subroutine

_(thread depth: 4)_

- **From:** Jared Thomas <jared@techie.com>
- **Date:** 1999-06-15T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3766CF06.6F569E02@techie.com>`
- **References:** `<7k3047$qo$1@clematis.singnet.com.sg> <19990614094123.04794.00001477@ng-fs1.aol.com> <7k3qcu$knc@dfw-ixnews12.ix.netcom.com> <7k6dr4$i3g$1@clematis.singnet.com.sg>`

```
The DCB can be changed to be RMODE(31).  Look in the newer DFP or
SFSMS books.  Basically, the DCB will point to a new control
block, the DCBE (DCB Extended) which will hold the 31 bita
addresses of EODAD and SYNAD routines.  Beyond that, there are no
changes.  PUT and GET work the same and all you do is re-assemble.


Goobsa C wrote:
> 
> Thanks for your suggestions.
…[10 more quoted lines elided]…
> <snip>
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: IBM COBOL calling Assembly subroutine

- **From:** William Lynch <WBLynch@att.net>
- **Date:** 1999-06-15T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3765E784.A53F7008@att.net>`
- **References:** `<7k3047$qo$1@clematis.singnet.com.sg> <19990614094123.04794.00001477@ng-fs1.aol.com>`

```
S Comstock wrote:
> 
> The I-would-like-help-but-I-don't-want-to-tell-you-who-I-am -named "News"
…[30 more quoted lines elided]…
> code so we can look at it.

This should work with AMODE(ANY) & RMODE(24) (as Steve indicated,
modules with 3-byte adcons must be below the 16MB line). If you have the
ASM source, maybe you can change the adcon from AL3(some_place) to
A(some_place). If the AL3 is being defined by a macro, be happy with
AMODE(ANY) & RMODE(24).

Bill Lynch
```

## Thread: Trimming Fields — 12 msgs

### Trimming Fields

- **From:** elmo_lover13@hotmail.com (Martina Dick)
- **Date:** 1999-06-14T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<r1493.1763$S7.880732@WReNphoon3>`

```
Does anybody know how to trim fields?

I've to transfer name details and need to lose the spaces between first name
and surname.

The first name is a field of 15 characters and the surname is 20 characters.
I need to make the new field be a concatenation of both with extra spaces
inbetween..

Example
First Name 'James          '
Surname 'Brown                    '

New Field 'James Brown'

Thanks!



   -**** Posted from RemarQ, http://www.remarq.com/?a ****-
 Search and Read Usenet Discussions in your Browser - FREE -
```

- **References:** `<r1493.1763$S7.880732@WReNphoon3>`

```
String Name-1 delimited by SPACES
           ' ' delimited by SIZE
           Name-2 delimited by SPACES
into FULL-NAME.

#### ↳ Re: Trimming Fields

- **From:** "Jerry Peacock" <bismail@bisusa.com>
- **Date:** 1999-06-14T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<mgj93.353$7G6.113431@typhoon01.swbell.net>`

```

Martina Dick <elmo_lover13@hotmail.com> wrote in message
news:r1493.1763$S7.880732@WReNphoon3...

I like:

MOVE SPACES TO NAMES.
UNSTRING FIRST-NAME DELIMITED ALL ' ' INTO
     NAME(1)
     NAME(2)
     NAME(3)
     NAME(4) etc to as many given-names as is possible, then

STRING NAME(1) DELIMITED SPACE
     ' ' DELIMITED SIZE
     NAME(2) DELIMITED ' '
    ' ' DELIMITED SIZE
     etc
     LAST-NAME DELIMITED SIZE INTO FINAL-OUTPUT.

P.S.
Glad to see you're doing your homework.
```

#### ↳ Re: Trimming Fields

- **From:** Rolf Klemenz <rolf.klemenz@ubs.com>
- **Date:** 1999-06-14T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3764D3C1.2AEB9EFC@ubs.com>`
- **References:** `<r1493.1763$S7.880732@WReNphoon3>`

```
Hi

Try STRING  NAME SURNAME 
	    DELIMITED BY ' ' 
 	    INTO NEWFIELD

Not shure, but should work.

Rolf

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Trimming Fields

- **From:** Ken Foskey <waratah@zip.com.au>
- **Date:** 1999-06-14T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3764EB93.2AD2EC40@zip.com.au>`
- **References:** `<r1493.1763$S7.880732@WReNphoon3> <3764D3C1.2AEB9EFC@ubs.com>`

```
Rolf Klemenz wrote:
> 
> Hi
> 
> Try STRING  first-NAME 
                 delimited by '  '
	      SURNAME
>             DELIMITED BY '  '
>             INTO NEWFIELD
> 

Always use two spaces as there is probably more than a single
name.  It will fail sometimes if the user enters two spaces
between names but is a good easy solution.

For a thorough job consider unstring into multiple character
arrays pulling the length of each field into a number variable. 
You can then peice together as many names as you have got without
any errors and guarantee a single space between every word.

	STRING word(1)(1:len-1) delimited by size
               ' ' delimited by size
               word(2) (1:len-2) delimited by size
               ......

Happy RTFM
Ken
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Trimming Fields

- **From:** Daniel Jacot <daniel.jacot@winterthur.ch>
- **Date:** 1999-06-14T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7k2tf6$nt8$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`
- **References:** `<r1493.1763$S7.880732@WReNphoon3> <3764D3C1.2AEB9EFC@ubs.com>`

```
An what about 'Jean Francis de la Plata' or 'Peter J. Brown Miller'.
Just 'Jean de' or 'Peter Brown'?
I'd search from the right to the left until first non-blank character:

PERFORM VARYING WS-IND1 FROM 20 BY -1 UNTIL
   SURNAME(WS-IND1:1) NOT = SPACES OR WS-IND1 = ZERO
END-PERFORM
...
STRING SURNAME(1:WS-IND1) DELIMITED BY SIZE,
       NAME(1:WS-IND2)    DELIMITED BY SIZE
  INTO FULL-NAME
END-STRING
...

(Left some details apart, don't want to make ALL of your homework ;-)

  rolf.klemenz@ubs.com wrote:

#### ↳ Re: Trimming Fields

- **From:** "Jonathan Robert" <jroberts@globalserve.net>
- **Date:** 1999-06-15T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<37666bf2$0$20691@fountain.mindlink.net>`
- **References:** `<r1493.1763$S7.880732@WReNphoon3>`

```
This bit of code takes f1 "James", f2 "Smith"
and makes it into "Smith, James".
Kinda similiar to what you want.

           Initialize Xx Yy Zz.
           Perform Test After Varying Xx from 1 By 1 Until Xx = 15
                   If Init-Lname (Xx:) = Space
                      Add 1 To Yy
                   End-If
           End-Perform
           Compute Zz = 15 - Yy
           Move Init-Lname (1:Zz) To Ws-Name(1:)
           Add 1 To Zz
           Move "," To Ws-Name(Zz:)
           Add 2 To Zz.
           Move Init-Fname To Ws-Name(Zz:).
           Move Ws-Name    To Main-EF-43-Value.
           Display Main-Ef-43-Screen.

Regards.

RBA

news:r1493.1763$S7.880732@WReNphoon3...

name
> and surname.

characters.

> inbetween..

Nobody has hit on the simple compress-in-place perform.

Redefine the data area as an indexed array and scan the array moving
each non-blank byte foward as needed (after encountering the first blank
byte).

Great for relief from "gas" pressure...(grin)...

spaces
> inbetween..

<a id="t-e5e731a0b9"></a>
## Thread: Student Requiring Help — 3 msgs

_Topics: [`Tutorials, books, learning`](topics.md#learning)_

### Student Requiring Help

- **From:** Ken Wilson <ken.wilson@home.com>
- **Date:** 1999-06-14T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<37654188.8680860A@home.com>`

```
I am a Computer Systems Technology student at Camosun College in
Victoria, BC, Canada.  I have a research paper to do on Cobol, in
particular showing its state of development for language constructs,
data types, etc. when compared with other common, and not so common,
programming languages.

In a nutshell, much search on the net has failed to find a definitive,
let alone useful, tutorial on the language.  Most go into how to program
in this language.  I am looking more for a tutorial on how the language
itself is built.

Any help whatsoever in this area would be greatly appreciated.  My
thanks in advance.

Ken Wilson
```

#### ↳ Re: Student Requiring Help

We are a group of PC developers in Australia and have been handed an
ASCII data file from a Cobol application running on a mainframe.  It
contains 'overpunched data'.  I haven't come across this before
(forgive my ignorance), but what exactly is overpunched data?  And how

responses to be e-mailed to me at:

info@techsol.com.au

Myles

=====================================================
Myles Wakeham
Tech Solutions (Aust) Pty Ltd
Adelaide, Australia
www.techsol.com.au
E-Mail:  info@techsol.com.au
```

#### ↳ Re: Help! Newbie question

- **From:** Barticus@att.spam.net (Randall Bart)
- **Date:** 1999-06-15T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3765a763.8958542@netnews>`
- **References:** `<0BC6B8896E20C66B.085B151946F48E4C.EF6B7769E6C6C067@lp.airnews.net>`

```
'Twas Mon, 14 Jun 1999 20:32:40 GMT, when info@techsol.com.au (Myles
Wakeham) illuminated comp.lang.cobol thusly:

> I don't hang on Usenet that much, so I'd really appreciate any
> responses to be e-mailed to me at:

I was about to answer your question, but you have committed not one but
two usenet faux pas.  First, the subject line of a message should indicate
what you are asking about.  The subject needs to contain at least one word
other than "help", "newbie", "question", or the topic of the newsgroup (in
this case it would be "COBOL").

Second, and more serious, there is a doctrine of Usenet "ask here, answer
here".  This newsgroup doesn't exist for just your benefit.  If it's worth
an answer, it's worth an answer in the newsgroup.  If you're not willing
to come back and look for the answer (just scan for the descriptive
subject which you used) then you're not participating.

So please come back and asky your question the right way, because I really
want to answer it.
```

## Thread: BARCODE - Code39, Using with Cobol and HP laserjet printer — 3 msgs

### BARCODE - Code39, Using with Cobol and HP laserjet printer

- **From:** Rich Johnson <rjohn61389@yahoo.com>
- **Date:** 1999-06-14T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<929405489.20577@www.remarq.com>`

```
I have to print Code39 barcodes using Microfocus Cobol
running on a Unix box, I have a Laserjet 4si printer with
barcode and more cartridge. Question is

How do I invoke the barcode to print. I have the Escape
sequence needed. I have a print program "shop traveler" that
I would like to barcode certain fields, ie Part Number,
order number etc.



**** Posted from RemarQ - http://www.remarq.com - Discussions Start Here (tm) ****
```

#### ↳ Re: BARCODE - Code39, Using with Cobol and HP laserjet printer

- **From:** "James King" <mangogwr@bellsouth.net>
- **Date:** 1999-06-14T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<iii93.13220$hh1.2939@news1.mia>`
- **References:** `<929405489.20577@www.remarq.com>`

```
As long as you can write to the Printer, then if you can 'embed' the
seqeunces within
the data, the printer will recognize them and do its thing.  The 'Esc'
sequence is called
the escape sequence because ASCII 27 / Hex 1b is the 'Escape' character
which means
to 'break out of what you are doing and go into a special mode',  as LONG AS
the characters
that follow the 'Esc' are coded correctly (and there are two parts) it will
work.
Hdr 'Esc'
Part 1: command code - one to several bytes that indicate what TYPE of
function to perform
Part 2: the values to be used

repeat as needed

e.g.  'ESC'+Elite_Font_Code+Font_Size+Font_Special (Bold/Italic,etc)


Rich Johnson wrote in message <929405489.20577@www.remarq.com>...
>I have to print Code39 barcodes using Microfocus Cobol
>running on a Unix box, I have a Laserjet 4si printer with
…[9 more quoted lines elided]…
>**** Posted from RemarQ - http://www.remarq.com - Discussions Start Here
(tm) ****
```

> 
> I have to print Code39 barcodes using Microfocus Cobol
…[8 more quoted lines elided]…
> **** Posted from RemarQ - http://www.remarq.com - Discussions Start Here (tm) ****

printing programmed yet.

find any,and decided to write my own.
I had some 5 1/4 diskettes with a lot of my old code, and
MS COBOL V2.20 COMPILER.(isam + runcob.exe)

#### ↳ Re: need help with ESCAPE KEYS and old COBOL

- **From:** Barticus@att.spam.net (Randall Bart)
- **Date:** 1999-06-15T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3765a5ad.8521229@netnews>`

'Twas Mon, 14 Jun 1999 06:49:14 GMT, when ac9341041@my-deja.com
illuminated comp.lang.cobol thusly:

> PROBLEMS  I can not make those programs accept any ESCAPE KEYS  Except
> Enter and Esc.
> most important to me will be to be able to use Pg. up, PG down and the
> Arrow Keys. Right now these keys cause my computer to beep and garbage
> to be displayed on the screen.

IIRC those keys return two bytes, just as though it's two keystrokes.  The
first one is zero, then the second one is a code indicating which key.  If
you do some experimenting you should be able to decode those values.

## Thread: Determingin LE Runtime options currently in force — 4 msgs

### Determingin LE Runtime options currently in force

- **From:** "Ian Reid" <reid.ian@pmintl.ch>
- **Date:** 1999-06-15T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** bit.listserv.cics-l,bit.listserv.ibm-main,comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<01beb713$e8ef6fa0$3000810a@pmecheew0267>`

```
Hello all,

I'm in a bit of a fix - 

Does anyone have a utility to *list* the current default LE runtime options
in use on a 
CICS system  ?

Also, what about a utility that will list any LE runtime options compiled
into CICS or 
Batch programs ?

Here's hoping

Ian Reid 
```

#### ↳ Re: Determingin LE Runtime options currently in force

- **From:** willjamu@mindspring.com (James A. Williams)
- **Date:** 1999-06-15T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** bit.listserv.cics-l,bit.listserv.ibm-main,comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<37662864.382114050@news.mindspring.com>`
- **References:** `<01beb713$e8ef6fa0$3000810a@pmecheew0267>`

```
"Ian Reid" <reid.ian@pmintl.ch> wrote:

IPCS has a VERBX called LEDATA that you can use if you have a dump.

For CICS its IP VERBX LEDATA  'CAA(XXXXXX) ALL'  I believe where
XXXXXX is from REG12 and has a valid CEECAA eye catcher. In batch you
have the RPTOPTS(ON) option that you can use. For batch you can also
specify LEDATA and the CAA is found automatically. IBM has said they
may create a CICS transaction to get the options in the future. If you
go to the LE www site there is a PDF file on debugging LE
applications.
>Hello all,
>
…[16 more quoted lines elided]…
>the same taken as individuals or companies
```

#### ↳ Re: Determingin LE Runtime options currently in force

- **From:** Daniel Jacot <daniel.jacot@winterthur.ch>
- **Date:** 1999-06-15T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** bit.listserv.cics-l,bit.listserv.ibm-main,comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7k5id4$l7m$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`
- **References:** `<01beb713$e8ef6fa0$3000810a@pmecheew0267>`

```
In article <01beb713$e8ef6fa0$3000810a@pmecheew0267>,
  "Ian Reid" <reid.ian@pmintl.ch> wrote:
> Hello all,
>
…[10 more quoted lines elided]…
> Ian Reid

AFAIK, there is no utility, but you can create your own options-module
and link it to an existing and running program. That options-module has
just one statement: RPTOPTS=ON. You the run the program and have a look
at the output:

In batch and IMS/TM, look at DD-name SYSOUT. If SYSOUT is not coded in
JCL, the file is allocated dynamically to SYSOUT=*.

In CICS, the report is written to transient data queue CESE. It is
prefixed with terminal-name, transaction-name and the current
timestamp, it may look like:

AE72AM02 19990615143232 Installation default      ABPERC(NONE)
AE72AM02 19990615143232 Installation default      ABTERMENC(ABEND)
AE72AM02 19990615143232 Installation default    NOAIXBLD
.....

You can use the following JCL to create a options-module into a
'&&-file' and link it to the application. You may need to change every
line marked with '+++++'. And... don't forget to replace the CEEUOPT if
you don't need the report anymore!

//* assemble the options-module:
//STEP1 EXEC PGM=ASMA90,PARM='DECK,NOOBJECT'
//SYSPRINT DD SYSOUT=*
//SYSUT1 DD UNIT=SYSDA,SPACE=(CYL,(1,1))
//SYSUT2 DD UNIT=SYSDA,SPACE=(CYL,(1,1))
//SYSUT3 DD UNIT=SYSDA,SPACE=(CYL,(1,1))
//SYSPUNCH DD DSN=&&TEMPOBJ(CEEUOPT),DISP=(,PASS),UNIT=SYSDA,
// SPACE=(TRK,(1,1,1)),DCB=(BLKSIZE=3120,LRECL=80,DSORG=PO)
//SYSLIB DD DSN=SCEE.SCEEMAC,DISP=SHR               +++++
//       DD DSN=SYS1.MACLIB,DISP=SHR                +++++
//SYSIN DD *
CEEUOPT  CSECT
CEEUOPT  AMODE ANY
CEEUOPT  RMODE ANY
         CEEXOPT RPTOPTS=(ON)
         END
/*
//  IF RC <= 4 THEN
//* link the options-module to the existing main-program
//STEP2    EXEC  PGM=IEWL,
//         PARM='NCAL,RENT,LIST,XREF,LET,MAP,SIZE=(9999K,96K)'
//SYSPRINT DD  SYSOUT=*
//SYSUT1   DD  UNIT=SYSDA,SPACE=(TRK,(5,5))
//SYSLMOD  DD  DSNAME=>>yourloadlib>>,DISP=SHR      +++++
//SYSLIB   DD  DSN=&&TEMPOBJ,DISP=(OLD,PASS)
//SYSLIN   DD  *
 REPLACE CEEUOPT
 INCLUDE SYSLMOD(>>yourmodule<<)                    +++++
 INCLUDE SYSLIB(CEEUOPT)
 ENTRY >>yourentry<<                                +++++
 NAME >>yourmodule<<(R)                             +++++
// ENDIF
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Determingin LE Runtime options currently in force

- **From:** "Dave" <saltaire@ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 1999-06-20T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** bit.listserv.cics-l,bit.listserv.ibm-main,comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7kif6n$ii0@dfw-ixnews3.ix.netcom.com>`
- **References:** `<01beb713$e8ef6fa0$3000810a@pmecheew0267> <7k5id4$l7m$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`

```
My notes on the subject are at work, but as I recall I used a simpler method
to list LE options in effect - at least for batch, not CICS.
I wrote a COBOL program which did nothing but a STOP RUN in the PROCEDURE
DIVISION. Ie,
   PROCEDURE DIVISION.
   STOP RUN.
Then I executed it and included LE runtime parameters in the EXEC card:
  //RUN        EXEC PGM=DUMPROG,PARM='/RPTOPTS(ON)'
  //STEPLIB  DD  DSN=MYLIB,DISP=SHR
  //               DD  DSN=CEE.SCEERUN,DISP=SHR       LE RUNTIME LIB
  //SYSOUT  DD SYSOUT=*
  //

This gave me a listing of the LE runtime options in SYSOUT.

Dave Morton
marspyrs@aol.com  (<== the only Email location I check)

Daniel Jacot wrote in message <7k5id4$l7m$1@nnrp1.deja.com>...
>In article <01beb713$e8ef6fa0$3000810a@pmecheew0267>,
>  "Ian Reid" <reid.ian@pmintl.ch> wrote:
…[77 more quoted lines elided]…
>Share what you know. Learn what you don't.
```

#### ↳ Re: SYS$TRNLNM in VAXCOBOL

- **From:** redsky@ibm.net (Thane Hubbell)
- **Date:** 1999-06-15T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<Jl0PnHJ5PvPd-pn2-r1Tmdr1Xknww@Dwight_Miller.iix.com>`
- **References:** `<fpx93.247$e9.107555@WReNphoon3>`

```
On Tue, 15 Jun 1999 19:02:32, jmahony@objectservices.com (John Mahony)
wrote:

> I am trying figure out the COBOL syntax to use the VMS System services
> SYS$TRNLNM.  I haven't programmed in COBOL in years and have no VAXCOBOL or
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
>

Type HELP at the VMS prompt, then type system_services, then 
$TRNLNM and it will give you complete help.  It looks something like 
this:

 SYSTEM_SERVICES

  $TRNLNM

       Returns information about a logical name.

       On Alpha systems, this service accepts 64-bit addresses.

       Format

         SYS$TRNLNM  [attr] ,tabnam ,lognam ,[acmode] ,[itmlst]

       C Prototype

         int sys$trnlnm  (unsigned int *attr, void *tabnam, void

                         *lognam, unsigned char *acmode, void 
*itmlst);

---------------------------
This might help some:

        call "sys$trnlnm" using
                by reference trnlnm-log-attr
                by descriptor "LNM$SYSTEM"
                by descriptor 
SYSLOCKED-LOGICAL-NAME(1:syslocked-log-nam-len)
                omitted
                by reference trnlnm-itemlist
                giving trnlnm-sys-call-result
        end-call.



-------------------------
Trust the computer industry to shorten "Year 2000" to Y2K.  It was 
this
kind of thinking that caused the problem in the first place.

Try a better search engine: http://www.google.com

Visit my updated website at
http://www.geocities.com/Eureka/2006/
```

#### ↳ Re: SYS$TRNLNM in VAXCOBOL

- **From:** "Phil Howell" <phowell@snowyhydro.com.au>
- **Date:** 1999-06-21T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7kksh7$kta$1@news.mel.aone.net.au>`
- **References:** `<fpx93.247$e9.107555@WReNphoon3>`

```

John Mahony wrote in message ...
>I am trying figure out the COBOL syntax to use the VMS System services
>SYS$TRNLNM.  I haven't programmed in COBOL in years and have no VAXCOBOL or
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
>

    01  lnm_itmlst.
        03  filler                      pic s9(4) comp value 255.
        03  filler                      pic s9(4) comp value
                                            external lnm$_string.
        03  filler                      pointer value reference ws_tmp_buf.
        03  filler                      pointer value reference
ws_tmp_buf_len.
*   Terminator
        03  filler                      pic s9(9) comp value 0.

 Buffer UTL_CHECK_APPL_AVAILABILITY.COB                Insert        Forward

    call "sys$trnlnm" using
        by reference lnm$m_case_blind
        by descriptor "LNM$SYSTEM"
        by descriptor ws_tmp_buf2(1:ws_tmp_buf2_len)
        by reference psl$c_exec
        by reference lnm_itmlst
        giving result_status

    if result_status = ss$_nolognam then
        move spaces to ws_tmp_buf
 Buffer UTL_CHECK_APPL_AVAILABILITY.COB                Insert        Forward
```
